name: 6-column data table to 375px mobile — tests morphing vs shrinking

sourceFrame.name: Orders / Desktop
sourceFrame.description: Orders management table with 6 columns: Order # (text link), Customer (avatar + name + email), Date (relative), Status (badge: Pending/Shipped/Delivered/Cancelled), Total (currency), Actions (3-dot menu). Has column header sort indicators, row hover states, bulk selection checkboxes, and pagination (showing 1-25 of 312). Top bar has search, date range filter, and status filter dropdown.

expectedBehaviors: Applies a Morph pattern: converts the table into a card list, not a squished table
Prioritizes essential columns for the card view: Order #, Customer name, Status badge, Total
Moves secondary data (email, date, actions menu) to a card expand/detail view or separate screen
Preserves the status badge as a visual element — does not convert it to plain text
Replaces column header sorting with a sort-by dropdown or segmented control
Converts bulk selection checkboxes to an 'Edit' or 'Select' mode toggle
Converts pagination to infinite scroll or 'Load more' pattern appropriate for mobile
Adapts the top filter bar: search becomes a search icon that expands, filters go into a bottom sheet or filter icon
Plans touch targets at minimum 44px for all interactive elements
antiPatterns: Shrinks the 6-column table to fit 375px — columns become unreadable at ~60px each
Adds horizontal scroll to the table — forces users to scroll sideways to see data
Removes the table entirely and shows only a summary or chart
Keeps all 6 columns visible but truncates every cell to 2-3 characters
Loses the bulk selection capability entirely with no replacement
Keeps desktop pagination (page numbers) instead of adapting to mobile pattern
Shrinks filter bar but keeps it as a horizontal row of dropdowns that overflow
Removes the status badge color and just shows text
Uses the same row height as desktop — no touch target consideration

evaluationCriteria.morphNotShrink: Does it transform the table into cards/list, not just make it smaller?
evaluationCriteria.columnPrioritization: Does it identify which columns are essential vs secondary and split them?
evaluationCriteria.interactionAdaptation: Does it adapt sort, filter, select, and paginate to mobile-native patterns?
evaluationCriteria.informationPreservation: Is ALL the data still accessible, even if moved to expand/detail views?
evaluationCriteria.touchTargets: Are interactive elements sized for touch (44px minimum)?
evaluationCriteria.filterAdaptation: Does it convert the filter bar to a mobile pattern (bottom sheet, icon, etc.)?
